root_cause.text: OkHttp's certificate chain cleaner failed to correctly build the verified certificate chain on certain Android versions because it relied on a custom manual implementation instead of querying Android's internal trust manager, potentially leading to certificate pinning bypasses or incorrect validation failures.
root_cause.evidence: The patch introduces `AndroidTrustRootIndex`, which uses reflection to invoke `findTrustAnchorByIssuerAndSignature` on Android's `X509TrustManager`, and replaces the custom `CertificateAuthorityCouncil` with a more robust `CertificateChainCleaner` backed by this new trust index implementation.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: OkHttp's certificate chain cleaner failed to correctly build the verified certificate chain on certain Android versions because it relied on a custom manual implementation instead of querying Android's internal trust manager, potentially leading to certificate pinning bypasses or incorrect validation failures.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch introduces `AndroidTrustRootIndex`, which uses reflection to invoke `findTrustAnchorByIssuerAndSignature` on Android's `X509TrustManager`, and replaces the custom `CertificateAuthorityCouncil` with a more robust `CertificateChainCleaner` backed by this new trust index implementation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor CertificateAuthorityCouncil to CertificateChainCleaner and update corresponding tests.
intent_record.what: Renamed CertificateAuthorityCouncil to CertificateChainCleaner, updated its constructor to use RealTrustRootIndex, and renamed normalizeCertificateChain to clean.
intent_record.how: Updates the test class to instantiate and call methods on the newly renamed CertificateChainCleaner class, maintaining the same test assertions.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor test to use the renamed CertificateChainCleaner class and its updated API.
intent_record.what: Replaced CertificateAuthorityCouncil instantiation and method call with CertificateChainCleaner and its clean method.
intent_record.how: Updates the test suite to align with the renamed class and updated API for cleaning certificate chains.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor test to use the newly renamed CertificateChainCleaner class and its corresponding methods.
intent_record.what: Replaced CertificateAuthorityCouncil with CertificateChainCleaner and updated the method call from normalizeCertificateChain to clean.
intent_record.how: Updates the test to use the newly refactored class and method names, maintaining the existing test assertion and logic.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update tests to use the refactored CertificateChainCleaner class instead of CertificateAuthorityCouncil.
intent_record.what: Replaced CertificateAuthorityCouncil and its normalizeCertificateChain method with CertificateChainCleaner and its clean method.
intent_record.how: Updates the test to use the new CertificateChainCleaner class and RealTrustRootIndex to process the certificate chain.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor tests to use the updated CertificateChainCleaner API.
intent_record.what: Replaced CertificateAuthorityCouncil with CertificateChainCleaner and RealTrustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Updates the test to use the new API for certificate chain cleaning.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update test to use the new CertificateChainCleaner API.
intent_record.what: Replaced the usage of CertificateAuthorityCouncil with CertificateChainCleaner and updated the method call from normalizeCertificateChain to clean.
intent_record.how: Updates the test case to reflect structural changes in the underlying certificate chain cleaning API.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update test to use the new CertificateChainCleaner class.
intent_record.what: Replaced CertificateAuthorityCouncil with CertificateChainCleaner and updated the method call.
intent_record.how: Updates the test to use the new CertificateChainCleaner class and its clean method instead of the old CertificateAuthorityCouncil.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add 'this.' prefix to instance variable assignment for code consistency and clarity.
intent_record.what: Added the 'this.' prefix to the instance variable assignment in the constructor.
intent_record.how: Does not change runtime behavior, only clarifies variable scoping.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add imports for TLS trust root indexing classes.
intent_record.what: Added import statements for AndroidTrustRootIndex, RealTrustRootIndex, and TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Makes these TLS-related classes available for use in the current file, enabling future certificate validation logic.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add trustRootIndex method to facilitate certificate trust root indexing from an X509TrustManager.
intent_record.what: Added a new public method trustRootIndex that instantiates a RealTrustRootIndex using the provided X509TrustManager.
intent_record.how: Provides a mechanism to extract and index the accepted certificate issuers from a trust manager for TLS certificate path validation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix X509TrustManager extraction for Google Play Services SSL provider.
intent_record.what: Added a fallback mechanism to extract SSLParameters from the Google Play Services Conscrypt provider if the default extraction fails.
intent_record.how: Allows the retrieval of the X509TrustManager when the SSLSocketFactory is provided by Google Play Services, preventing failures on devices using this specific SSL provider.

intent_record.intent_summary: Override trustRootIndex to utilize Android-specific trust root extraction when possible.
intent_record.what: Added an overridden trustRootIndex method that attempts to retrieve an AndroidTrustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Checks if an Android-specific trust root index is available for the given trust manager, returning it if found, or falling back to the superclass implementation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update internal TLS imports to reflect the replacement of CertificateAuthorityCouncil with CertificateChainCleaner and TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.what: Replaced the import for CertificateAuthorityCouncil with CertificateChainCleaner and TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Allows the class to compile and use the newly restructured internal TLS certificate validation classes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor certificate chain cleaning to use the new CertificateChainCleaner API.
intent_record.what: Replaced the use of certificateAuthorityCouncil and normalizeCertificateChain with TrustRootIndex and CertificateChainCleaner.
intent_record.how: Updates the certificate chain cleaning mechanism prior to certificate pinning checks to use the new cleaner API.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor the SSL socket factory cache to use Platform-provided TrustRootIndex instead of CertificateAuthorityCouncil.
intent_record.what: Replaced CertificateAuthorityCouncil with TrustRootIndex and updated the caching method to use Platform-provided trustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Updates the static cache to store and return a TrustRootIndex obtained from the Platform instead of manually instantiating a CertificateAuthorityCouncil.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add Android-specific TrustRootIndex utilizing reflection for faster initialization.
intent_record.what: Adds a new AndroidTrustRootIndex class.
intent_record.how: Provides an implementation of TrustRootIndex that uses reflection to find trusted root certificates via Android's X509TrustManager.

intent_record.intent_summary: Refactor certificate chain normalization by extracting root indexing into TrustRootIndex and renaming to CertificateChainCleaner.
intent_record.what: Renamed CertificateAuthorityCouncil to CertificateChainCleaner and replaced its internal certificate mapping with a TrustRootIndex dependency.
intent_record.how: Decouples the trust root indexing logic from the chain cleaning logic by delegating CA lookups to the injected TrustRootIndex.

intent_record.intent_summary: Delegate trusted CA certificate lookup and verification to the trustRootIndex.
intent_record.what: Changed the method call to use trustRootIndex and removed the secondary signature verification check.
intent_record.how: Delegates certificate lookup to trustRootIndex, which assumes responsibility for verifying the signature, thereby simplifying the local condition.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove dead code for finding CA certificates by issuer and signature.
intent_record.what: Removed the findByIssuerAndSignature method from the CertificateAuthorityCouncil class.
intent_record.how: Eliminates the logic that searches for and verifies a trusted CA certificate by its issuer and signature, likely because it is unused or has been moved elsewhere.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add RealTrustRootIndex to efficiently look up and verify trusted root certificates.
intent_record.what: Added a new class RealTrustRootIndex that implements TrustRootIndex to index X509Certificates by their subject X500Principal.
intent_record.how: Allows efficient lookup and verification of an issuer certificate for a given X509Certificate by mapping subject names to lists of potential CA certificates and verifying their signatures.

intent_record.intent_summary: Introduce the TrustRootIndex interface to abstract trusted CA certificate lookups.
intent_record.what: Added a new Java interface file for TrustRootIndex.
intent_record.how: Defines a contract for finding a trusted CA certificate by issuer and signature.

grouping_result.intent: Remove dead code for finding CA certificates by issuer and signature.
Add 'this.' prefix to instance variable assignment for code consistency and clarity.
Introduce the TrustRootIndex interface to abstract trusted CA certificate lookups.
Fix X509TrustManager extraction for Google Play Services SSL provider.
Refactor the SSL socket factory cache to use Platform-provided TrustRootIndex instead of CertificateAuthorityCouncil.

consistency_review.core_intent: Refactor CertificateAuthorityCouncil into CertificateChainCleaner and TrustRootIndex, updating related tests and removing obsolete code.
Introduce the TrustRootIndex interface and its related implementations to abstract trusted CA certificate lookups.

relevance_review.reason: The group refactors the certificate chain cleaning logic into CertificateChainCleaner and TrustRootIndex, which directly relates to the root cause involving the certificate chain cleaner's implementation.
The group only contains a code style change (adding 'this.' prefix) which is completely unrelated to the certificate chain cleaner vulnerability.

The group introduces a TrustRootIndex interface and Android-specific implementations to correctly extract and verify trusted root certificates, addressing the failure in the custom certificate chain cleaner.

The group directly addresses the extraction of the X509TrustManager for the Google Play Services SSL provider, which is required to fix the certificate chain cleaner's reliance on Android's internal trust manager.

The group replaces the custom CertificateAuthorityCouncil with the Platform-provided TrustRootIndex, directly addressing the vulnerability's root cause of relying on a custom manual implementation instead of the platform's trust manager.

candidates.rationale: This line relies on the custom manual implementation to find the trusted CA certificate instead of using Android's internal trust manager.
Calls the custom certificate chain builder which does not properly query Android's internal trust manager.
Instantiates the custom manual certificate chain cleaner instead of using Android's internal trust manager.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement represents the custom manual implementation used to find the trusted CA certificate instead of delegating to Android's internal trust manager, which caused the certificate chain verification to fail or be bypassed on certain Android versions.
This statement calls the custom certificate chain builder (CertificateAuthorityCouncil) which relies on the flawed manual implementation rather than the platform's trust manager.
This statement instantiates the custom manual certificate chain cleaner instead of using Android's internal trust manager.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Start using CertificateAuthorityCouncil in CertificatePinner.

The wiring is definitely a bit more awkward than I would
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 62b42bf27589fd8128f6d1a202455c5731c890c2
Author: jwilson <jwilson@squareup.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 1 20:02:07 2016 -0500

    Start using CertificateAuthorityCouncil in CertificatePinner.
    
    The wiring is definitely a bit more awkward than I would like. The
    CertificateAuthorityCountil is a non-public class, and this change
    sneaks an instance into the CertificatePinner that couples it to
    the OkHttp client's SSLSocketFactory.
    
    A nicer solution is to expose Certificate
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the use of 'CertificateAuthorityCouncil' to manually normalize the certificate chain ('lastCertificateAuthorityCouncil = new CertificateAuthorityCouncil(trustManager.getAcceptedIssuers());' and '.normalizeCertificateChain(unverifiedHandshake.peerCertificates())'). This manual custom implementation is the root cause of the vulnerability, as it fails to properly utilize Android's internal trust manager to build the verified chain.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: First draft of the new new stream allocations model.
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit c358656c8799d30fd422448153e99a5dd37e298a
Author: jwilson <jwilson@squareup.com>
Date:   Fri Nov 27 19:16:06 2015 -0500

    First draft of the new new stream allocations model.

diff --git a/okhttp/src/main/java/com/squareup/okhttp/internal/io/RealConnection.java b/okhttp/src/main/java/com/squareup/okhttp/internal/io/RealConnection.java
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..f2e443a1e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/okhttp/src/main/java/com/squareup/okhttp/internal/io/RealConnection.java
@@ -0,0 +1,35
bic_trace_step.reason: The connectTls method directly passes the raw, uncleaned peer certificates from the handshake (unverifiedHandshake.peerCertificates()) to the CertificatePinner, without properly cleaning the chain using Android's TrustManager. This lacks the correct certificate chain cleaning logic.

Start using CertificateAuthorityCouncil in CertificatePinner.

The wiring is definitely a bit more awkward than I would
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 62b42bf27589fd8128f6d1a202455c5731c890c2
Author: jwilson <jwilson@squareup.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 1 20:02:07 2016 -0500

    Start using CertificateAuthorityCouncil in CertificatePinner.
    
    The wiring is definitely a bit more awkward than I would like. The
    CertificateAuthorityCountil is a non-public class, and this change
    sneaks an instance into the CertificatePinner that couples it to
    the OkHttp client's SSLSocketFactory.
    
    A nicer solution is to expose Certificate
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces `CertificateAuthorityCouncil` to normalize the certificate chain (`normalizeCertificateChain(unverifiedHandshake.peerCertificates())`). This is the custom manual implementation initialized via `trustManager.getAcceptedIssuers()` that fails on certain Android versions, rather than delegating to Android's internal trust manager.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: First draft of the new new stream allocations model.
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit c358656c8799d30fd422448153e99a5dd37e298a
Author: jwilson <jwilson@squareup.com>
Date:   Fri Nov 27 19:16:06 2015 -0500

    First draft of the new new stream allocations model.

diff --git a/okhttp/src/main/java/com/squareup/okhttp/internal/io/RealConnection.java b/okhttp/src/main/java/com/squareup/okhttp/internal/io/RealConnection.java
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..f2e443a1e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/okhttp/src/main/java/com/squareup/okhttp/internal/io/RealConnection.java
@@ -0,0 +1,35
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `connectTls` passes the uncleaned certificate chain directly to the CertificatePinner via `address.getCertificatePinner().check(address.getUriHost(), unverifiedHandshake.peerCertificates());`. There is no logic to correctly build or clean the verified certificate chain using Android's internal trust manager before pinning validation.
